div.head: CR–14. Editorial Note
div.p:
The International Bank for Reconstruction and Development authorized the following loans to Costa Rica during the years 1958–1960: (1) $3.5 million to the Central Bank of Costa Rica on February 11, 1959, to relend for agricultural and light industrial development. (2) $2 million to the Central Bank of Costa Rica on May 4, 1960, to finance loans to industrial firms for importing capital goods. (International Bank for Reconstruction and Development,
Fourteenth Annua1 Report, 1958–1959, page 30; and Fifteenth Annual Report, 1959–1960, page 32)

The Export-Import Bank authorized a credit of $3 million to Costa Rica on April 15, 1960 for Inter-American Highway construction, but no funds were disbursed in 1960. For additional information, see Export-Import Bank of Washington, Report to the Congress for the Twelve Months Ending June 30, 1961, page 144.
